title: alnus firma subsp. hirtella
intro: Alnus firma subsp. hirtella, also known as Japanese alder or green alder, is a subspecies of deciduous tree native to East Asia. The bark of this plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Alnus firma subsp. hirtella include reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and soothing skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the bark can be made into a decoction or infusion to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
